data.narrative:
Cambridge Aerospace, a Series A–stage defense and security company that has raised $136M to date, sits within a competitive cohort of 10 private peers and 2 public comparables. The private peer set includes Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries at the same stage, alongside later-stage players like Anduril Industries and Scale AI.

Two valuation methods triangulate Cambridge's fair value. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $136M raised, implies $272M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M) yields a median of $300M, with a range of $150M–$500M. These methods produce a median valuation of $286M, with a low-bound estimate of $231M and high-bound of $345M.

The current stated valuation of approximately $1B diverges materially from triangulated support. Key uncertainties include limited transparency on founder pedigree, the competitive intensity within the Series A defense cohort, and timing risk inherent in an active $200M fundraise. The $231M–$345M range represents defensible support based on stage and peer positioning, though ultimate pricing will reflect investor sentiment on execution risk and market timing.
